TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Empire of Men Faction.

Translations.Comment: Multiple kingdoms. Plural 'Imperi' maintains grand, multi-kingdom nature of human empire faction.
Skilled swordsman. 'Spadaccino' conveys skilled warrior; 'Imperiale' emphasizes imperial service.
Dismounted knights. Clearly indicates on-foot knights, distinguishing from cavalry units.
Halberd professionals. 'Alabardiere' is professional Italian term for halberd-wielders.
Spear formations. 'Lanciere' emphasizes pike/lance-based formations of imperial armies.
Heavy cavalry elite. Direct translation emphasizing mainstay of imperial power.
Swift scouts. Standard term for agile cavalry skirmishers and reconnaissance units.
Ranged support. Classic term emphasizing archery support for formations.
Crossbow specialists. More heavily armed and powerful than basic archers.
Arquebusiers. Specialists in Renaissance-era firearms with imperial training.
Siege artillery. Direct translation emphasizing heavy firepower of empire.
Indirect artillery. Provides indirect fire support for imperial forces.
Massive war machine. 'Titano' conveys colossal, intimidating power of war engine.
Heraldic symbol. Majestic creature representing imperial power and majesty.
Ultimate flyer. Commands respect and awe; supreme aerial weapon of empire.
Military rank. Seasoned battle commander and leader of troops.
Military authority. Emphasizes command over mounted knights and knightly orders.
Magic user. Emphasizes magical prowess and arcane support for empire.
